title: ⚔️ Chapter Boss ⭐⭐⭐: Past Continuous vs Past Simple

questions.prompt: Complete: "When I ___ into the room, the children were sleeping."

options.text: walked
was walking
were walking
walk

questions.explanation: The short action introduced by when takes the past simple: When I walked into the room… The long background action (the children were sleeping) is the continuous one. "was/were walking" wrongly makes the short event continuous, and "walk" is the base form.

options.text: They were playing chess when I arrived.
While we were waiting, it started to rain.
I was knowing the answer immediately.
She was cooking dinner at seven o'clock.

questions.explanation: The error is (c): know is a stative verb and is not used in the continuous — it should be I knew the answer immediately. (a), (b) and (d) all use the past continuous correctly with action verbs (play, wait, cook).

questions.prompt: Complete: "I was reading a book when the lights ___."

options.text: were going out
went out
go out
was going out

questions.explanation: The sudden, short event that interrupts the long action takes the past simple: …when the lights went out. The background action (was reading) is continuous, but the interruption is simple, so "were/was going out" and the present "go out" are wrong.

options.text: While she studied, the phone was ringing.
While she studying, the phone rang.
While she was studying, the phone was rang.
While she was studying, the phone rang.

questions.explanation: while marks the long action in the past continuous and the short interruption is past simple: While she was studying, the phone rang. (a) swaps the tenses, (b) drops was, and (c) wrongly puts the short event in a broken continuous form ("was rang").

questions.prompt: Complete: "At that moment, I ___ what to say, so I stayed silent."

options.text: wasn't knowing
didn't know
weren't knowing
am not knowing

questions.explanation: know is a stative verb, so it stays simple even at a past moment: I didn't know what to say. The continuous forms "wasn't/weren't knowing" and the present "am not knowing" are all wrong because state verbs are not used in the continuous.

options.text: He hurt his leg while he was playing football.
We were having lunch when the news came on.
I was wanting an ice cream all afternoon.
The baby was crying when the doctor came in.

questions.explanation: The error is (c): want is a stative verb and is not used in the continuous — it should be I wanted an ice cream. (a), (b) and (d) correctly pair a long action in the past continuous with a short past-simple event.
